devanagari: भगवता हंसरूपेण ब्रह्मणे ज्ञानोपदेशः । श्रीभगवानुवाच । सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणा बुद्धेर्न च आत्मनः । सत्त्वेनान्यतमौ हन्यात् सत्त्वं सत्त...
english_devnagari: bhagavatā haṃsarūpeṇa brahmaṇe jñānopadeśaḥ śrībhagavānuvāca sattvaṃ rajastama iti guṇā buddherna ca ātmanaḥ sattvenānyatamau hanyāt sattvaṃ sattvena caiva hi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
Sattva, Rajas and Tamas are the attributes of the Intellect (Buddhi called here as Prakṛti) and not of the Self. Therefore by (re-inforcing the attribute) Sattva, one should overcome (the tendencies of) the other two (viz. Rajas and Tamas) and then by developing Sattva (in the form of perfect tranquility) one should subdue Sattva (characterised by virtues like truthfulness, etc.)

devanagari: सत्त्वात् धर्मो भवेद्‍ वृद्धात् पुंसो मद्‍भक्तिलक्षणः । सात्त्विकोपासया सत्त्वं ततो धर्मः प्रवर्तते ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sattvāt dharmo bhaved‍ vṛddhāt puṃso mad‍bhaktilakṣaṇaḥ sāttvikopāsayā sattvaṃ tato dharmaḥ pravartate
translation: When Sattva becomes dominant, it develops in Man dharma which is characterised by devotion to me. Sattva develops by use of Sāttvika things and then leads to dharma which is of the nature of Devotion to God.

devanagari: धर्मो रजस्तमो हन्यात् सत्त्ववृद्धिः अनुत्तमः । आशु नश्यति तन्मूलो ह्यधर्म उभये हते ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmo rajastamo hanyāt sattvavṛddhiḥ anuttamaḥ āśu naśyati tanmūlo hyadharma ubhaye hate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ han class 1 verb], [locative single from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [locative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ han class 2 verb], [locative single from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Righteousness characterised by devotion to me is the best form of virtue. It enhances the potency of Sattva which eliminates Rajas and Tamas. When these two are weeded out, unrighteousness which has its roots in these two is automatically exterminated.

devanagari: आगमोपः प्रजा देशः कालः कर्म च जन्म च । ध्यानं मंत्रोऽथ संस्कारो दशैते गुणहेतवः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: āgamopaḥ prajā deśaḥ kālaḥ karma ca janma ca dhyānaṃ maṃtro
translation: [1] Scriptures, water, progeny, sacred places, time, acts, birth, meditation, mantras and purificatory rites—these ten factors are the determinants in promoting a guṇa.

devanagari: तत् तत् सात्त्विकमेवैषां यद् यद् वृद्धाः प्रचक्षते । निन्दन्ति तामसं तत्तद् राजसं तद् उपेक्षितम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tat tat sāttvikamevaiṣāṃ yad yad vṛddhāḥ pracakṣate nindanti tāmasaṃ tattad rājasaṃ tad upekṣitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vṛdh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ nind class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ nind class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ nind class 1 verb]

translation: (Out of these) whatever is approved of as Sāttvika by persons expert in Śāslras and of mature judgement is (reliably) Sāttvika; (the acts, etc.) that they censure is Tāmasa and that about which they arc indifferent is Rājasa.

devanagari: सात्त्विकान्येव सेवेत पुमान् सत्त्वविवृद्धये । ततो धर्मस्ततो ज्ञानं यावत् स्मृतिरपोहनम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: sāttvikānyeva seveta pumān sattvavivṛddhaye tato dharmastato jñānaṃ yāvat smṛtirapohanam
translation: For the purpose of development of Sattva guṇa, one should use Sāttvika things.[2] Sattva guṇa leads to righteousness from which spiritual wisdom arises and it (goes on developing till it) results in the attainment of Self realization which eliminates ignorance and guṇas whereby the gross and subtle bodies come into being.[3]

devanagari: वेणुसङ्घर्षजो वह्निः दग्ध्वा शाम्यति तद्वनम् । एवं गुणव्यत्ययजो देहः शाम्यति तत्क्रियः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: veṇusaṅgharṣajo vahniḥ dagdhvā śāmyati tadvanam evaṃ guṇavyatyayajo dehaḥ śāmyati tatkriyaḥ
translation: Just as forest-conflagration caused by the friction of bamboos gets extinguished after completely burning down the bamboo forest, in the same manner this body which is the product of the unequal forces of guṇas ceases to be by the mutual friction and destruction of the guṇas.

devanagari: श्रीउद्धव उवाच । विदन्ति मर्त्याः प्रायेण विषयान् पदमापदाम् । तथापि भुञ्जते कृष्ण तत्कथं श्वखराजवत् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīuddhava uvāca vidanti martyāḥ prāyeṇa viṣayān padamāpadām tathāpi bhuñjate kṛṣṇa tatkathaṃ śvakharājavat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ vid class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 2 verb]

translation:
Uddhava said:
How is it that people indulge into enjoyment of senseobjects like a dog or a mule or a goat, even though most of the mortals generally know that enjoyment of such objects is the source of calamities.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । अहमित्यन्यथा बुद्धिः प्रमत्तस्य यथा हृदि । उत्सर्पति रजो घोरं ततो वैकारिकं मनः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca ahamityanyathā buddhiḥ pramattasya yathā hṛdi utsarpati rajo ghoraṃ tato vaikārikaṃ manaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord replied:
The perverted notion[4] of I-ness (identifying the body with the soul) springs up in the heart of a deluded person with such a force that the terrible rajas takes (complete) possession of the mind though (originally) it (the mind) is born of Sattva.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
That one is an independent agent of an action—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: रजोयुक्तस्य मनसः सङ्कल्पः सविकल्पकः । ततः कामो गुणध्यानाद् दुःसहः स्याद् हि दुर्मतेः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: rajoyuktasya manasaḥ saṅkalpaḥ savikalpakaḥ tataḥ kāmo guṇadhyānād duḥsahaḥ syād hi durmateḥ
translation: In the mind of a person bedevilled by the Rajas, arises first the notion of enjoyability of an object and then the special enjoyable features (and thoughts about the ways and means of attaining them) are thoroughly rooted in it. Ultimately owing to constant thinking on the excellent aspects of the objects, an irresistible passion for them is engendered in the mind of men of such perverted notion.

devanagari: करोति कामवशगः कर्माण्यविजितेन्द्रियः । दुःखोदर्काणि संपश्यन् रजोवेग विमोहितः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: karoti kāmavaśagaḥ karmāṇyavijitendriyaḥ duḥkhodarkāṇi saṃpaśyan rajovega vimohitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śā class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ śā class 4 verb]

translation: Being completely diluted (and swept off) by the vehemence of the Rajas, he loses his control over his senses and organs and being at the mercy of his carnal desires, rushes into acts even though he clearly foresees their painful consequences.

devanagari: रजस्तमोभ्यां यदपि विद्वान् विक्षिप्तधीः पुनः । अतंद्रितो मनो युञ्जन् दोषदृष्टिर्न सज्जते ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: rajastamobhyāṃ yadapi vidvān vikṣiptadhīḥ punaḥ ataṃdrito mano yuñjan doṣadṛṣṭirna sajjate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [vocative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: Although a man of knowledge and wisdom may get his intellect distracted and overpowered by Rajas and Tamas, he should assiduously and with alertness control his mind and realizing the evil consequences (of the enjoyment of such objects) should not be attached to them.

devanagari: अप्रमत्तोऽनुयुञ्जीत मनो मय्यर्पयन् शनैः । अनिर्विण्णो यथाकालं जितश्वासो जितासनः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: apramatto anirviṇṇo yathākālaṃ jitaśvāso jitāsanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛ ], [vocative single from √ ṛ ]

translation: Having achieved steadiness or bodily posture and control over the breath, one should, at the proper fixed time, gradually concentrate his mind on Me with vigilance and without feeling despondent (in case of distractions).

devanagari: एतावान् योग आदिष्टो मच्छिष्यैः सनकादिभिः । सर्वतो मन आकृष्य मय्यद्धाऽवेश्यते यथा ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvān yoga ādiṣṭo macchiṣyaiḥ sanakādibhiḥ sarvato mana ākṛṣya mayyaddhā
translation: The means whereby the mind can be withdrawn from all other objects and can be easily and properly concentrated in me has been proclaimed by me through my disciples like Sanaka and others.

devanagari: श्रीउद्धव उवाच । यदा त्वं सनकादिभ्यो येन रूपेण केशव । योगमादिष्टवानेतद् रूपमिच्छामि वेदितुम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīuddhava uvāca yadā tvaṃ sanakādibhyo yena rūpeṇa keśava yogamādiṣṭavānetad rūpamicchāmi veditum

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[locative single from √ u class 1 verb], [locative single from √ u class 2 verb], [locative single from √ u class 5 verb]

[nominative dual from √ u class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ u class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ u class 1 verb], [locative single from √ u class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ u class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ u class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ u class 2 verb], [locative single from √ u class 2 verb], [nominative dual from √ u class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ u class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ u class 5 verb], [locative single from √ u class 5 verb]

[nominative dual from √ u class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ u class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ u class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ u class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ u class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ u class 2 verb], [nominative dual from √ u class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ u class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ u class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ u class 5 verb]

translation:
Uddhava submitted:
I wish to know the form you assumed when you imparted the knowledge of this Toga to Sanaka and others (as they were earliest in creation and as such much anterior to you who are born at the fag end of Dvāpara.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । पुत्रा हिरण्यगर्भस्य मानसाः सनकादयः । पप्रच्छुः पितरं सूक्ष्मां योगस्यैकान्तिकीं गतिम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca putrā hiraṇyagarbhasya mānasāḥ sanakādayaḥ papracchuḥ pitaraṃ sūkṣmāṃ yogasyaikāntikīṃ gatim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord replied:
Sanaka and others, the mind-born sons of god Brahmā asked their father about the subtle and the ultimate stage of yoga[5] (or the knowledge or realization of the Lord).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
About the subtle process of Yoga and the nature of the Soul (or the Lord)—EK.

devanagari: सनकादय ऊचुः । गुणेष्वाविशते चेतो गुणाश्चेतसि च प्रभो । कथमन्योन्य संत्यागो मुमुक्षोः अतितितीर्षोः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sanakādaya ūcuḥ guṇeṣvāviśate ceto guṇāścetasi ca prabho kathamanyonya saṃtyāgo mumukṣoḥ atititīrṣoḥ
translation:
Sanaka and others submilted
O Lord I The mind naturally gets attached (and enters) the objects of enjoyment, while the objects (so enjoyed) enter into the mind (by implanting themselves as latencies). How can a person wishing to transcend them both in order to get himself liberated from Saṃsāra divorce them mutually?

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । एवं पृष्टो महादेवः स्वयंभूः भूतभावनः । ध्यायमानः प्रश्नबीजं नाभ्यपद्यत कर्मधीः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca evaṃ pṛṣṭo mahādevaḥ svayaṃbhūḥ bhūtabhāvanaḥ dhyāyamānaḥ praśnabījaṃ nābhyapadyata karmadhīḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
At this submission of the problem, the great self- created god Brahmā, the Creator of all beings, contemplated deeply over it, but as his mind was engrossed in the activity of creation, he could not pin-point the root of the question.

devanagari: स मामचिन्तयद् देवः प्रश्नपारतितीर्षया । तस्याहं हंसरूपेण सकाशमगमं तदा ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa māmacintayad devaḥ praśnapāratitīrṣayā tasyāhaṃ haṃsarūpeṇa sakāśamagamaṃ tadā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperfect active first single], [aorist active first single]

translation: With the desire of knowing the solution of the problem (lit. going to the other end of the question) god Brahmadeva concentrated his mind on me. Assuming the form of a swan[6] I presented myself near him.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Just as a swan has the capability to separate milk from water (if a mixture of the two is presented to it), the Lord went there to show them the process of separating the object of senses from the mind—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा मां त उपव्रज्य कृत्वा पादाभिवन्दनम् । ब्रह्मामग्रतः कृत्वा पप्रच्छुः को भवान् इति ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā māṃ ta upavrajya kṛtvā pādābhivandanam brahmāmagrataḥ kṛtvā papracchuḥ ko bhavān iti
translation: Seeing me, they approached me and bowed my feet, with god Brahmā as their leader, they enquired of me (through Brahmā) ‘Who are You?’

devanagari: इत्यहं मुनिभिः पृष्टः तत्त्वजिज्ञासुभिस्तदा । यदवोचमहं तेभ्यः तद् उद्धव निबोध मे ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ityahaṃ munibhiḥ pṛṣṭaḥ tattvajijñāsubhistadā yadavocamahaṃ tebhyaḥ tad uddhava nibodha me
translation: When I was thus accosted by the sages who were eager to know the truth, please listen attentively from me, O Uddhava, what I explained to them.

devanagari: वस्तुनो यद्यनानात्वमात्मनः प्रश्न ईदृशः । कथं घटेत वो विप्रा वक्तुर्वा मे क आश्रयः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: vastuno yadyanānātvamātmanaḥ praśna īdṛśaḥ kathaṃ ghaṭeta vo viprā vakturvā me ka āśrayaḥ
translation: [7] If there be non-difference or non-multiplicity in the reality (i.e. the real entity) of the Soul (and if you are putting the question with reference to the Soul), how can such a question arise, O Brāhmaṇas, and on what basis can I reply (as due to one-ness of Soul, I cease to be a distinct entity).
purport:
Footnote [7]:
(i) The question “Who are you” is untenable as both sentient and non-seṅticnt entities and the Paramātman are one. For I am the Inner- Controller of you all—The questioners.
(evam praṣṭṛ-ṅāṃ yuṣmākam apyaham antarāmte'tyuktam bhavati)
—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) If in your view, the reality about Paramātman be one (not- many) how can the question “Who are you” be tenable? If you presume manyness of the reality, the use of the singular number to many is untenable.
(anekasmin bahutva-saṃkhyā-viśiṣṭe vastuni ekatvasaṅkhyā-viśiṣṭatvena vaktum anupapannatvāt)

devanagari: पञ्चात्मकेषु भूतेषु समानेषु च वस्तुतः । को भवानिति वः प्रश्नो वाचारम्भो ह्यनर्थकः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: pañcātmakeṣu bhūteṣu samāneṣu ca vastutaḥ ko bhavāniti vaḥ praśno vācārambho hyanarthakaḥ
translation: [8] (If your question is relating to this physical body of mine it is equally meaningless). When, in reality (from the point of the constituents of the physical bodies of all beings) all beings are the same substance inasmuch as they are constituted of the same five gross elements. Hence the question “Who are you” is merely a verbal expression signifying nothing.[9]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
All the five categories of beings viz., gods, human beings, manes (pitṛs), Gandharvas and Asuras are inferior to Hiraṇyagarbha and arc not fit to be worshipped and as such arc equals. Owing to this uniqueness of mine whom god Brahmā and others worship, this question is superfluous

Footnote [9]:
vācārambhaṇaṃ vikāro nāmadheyam—Chāndogya Up. 6.1-4

devanagari: मनसा वचसा दृष्ट्या गृह्यतेऽन्यैः अपीन्द्रियैः । अहमेव न मत्तोऽन्यत् इति बुध्यध्वमञ्जसा ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: manasā vacasā dṛṣṭyā gṛhyate ahameva na matto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative middle second plural], [imperative passive second plural]

translation: Please grasp this firmly that whatever is perceived by mind, expressed by speech of apprehended by sight or by any other sense is Me and nothing else but Myself

devanagari: गुणेषु आविशते चेतो गुणाश्चेतसि च प्रजाः । जीवस्य देह उभयं गुणाश्चेतो मदात्मनः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇeṣu āviśate ceto guṇāścetasi ca prajāḥ jīvasya deha ubhayaṃ guṇāśceto madātmanaḥ
translation: [10] The mind enters (gets enmeshed) in the objects of senses and the objects (permanently) imprint (their latencies) on the mind and both the objects and the mind constitute the body of the jīva whose soul is I myself, O my (grand) sons.[11]
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Both guṇas (objects of senses) and the mind get burnt by the fire of knowledge—Padaratnāvalī

Footnote [11]:
The combination of the objects of senses and the mind which constitutes the body is an adjunct or the limiting condition superimposed on jīva which is essentially Brahman—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: गुणेषु च आविशत् चित्तमभीक्ष्णं गुणसेवया । गुणाश्च चित्तप्रभवा मद्रूप उभयं त्यजेत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇeṣu ca āviśat cittamabhīkṣṇaṃ guṇasevayā guṇāśca cittaprabhavā madrūpa ubhayaṃ tyajet
translation: [12] Realizing my one-ness with him, the jīva should renounce (and free himself from the bonds of) both the mind which gets involved into sense-objects by constantly indulging into them and the sense-objects born of the mind which leave their imprints on it. (in the form of reminiscences).
purport:
Footnote [12]:
It is by contemplation of my essential nature that both arc divorced from each other,
madrūpe cintyamāne sati...cittaṃ guṇāṃstyajati guṇāś cittaṃ tyajanti—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: जाग्रत् स्वप्नः सुषुप्तं च गुणतो बुद्धिवृत्तयः । तासां विलक्षणो जीवः साक्षित्वेन विनिश्चितः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: jāgrat svapnaḥ suṣuptaṃ ca guṇato buddhivṛttayaḥ tāsāṃ vilakṣaṇo jīvaḥ sākṣitvena viniścitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jāgṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ jāgṛ class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ jāgṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ jāgṛ class 2 verb]

translation: Wakefulness, the dream-state and deep sleep are the three states of the mind caused by the (dominance of) three guṇas,[13] the jīva, being the witness of these states, is conclusively determined to be distinct from them.
purport:
Footnote [13]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā quotes an authority which attributes wakefulness to sattva, the dream-state to rajas and deep sleep to tamas.

devanagari: यर्हि संसृतिबन्धोऽयं आत्मनो गुणवृत्तिदः । मयि तुर्ये स्थितो जह्यात् त्यागः तद् गुणचेतसाम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: yarhi saṃsṛtibandho mayi turye sthito jahyāt tyāgaḥ tad guṇacetasām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation: [14] As this bondage is superimposed on the Ātman by Buddhi (mind or intellect) and is thus the cause of saṃsāra (involvement in the cycle of births and deaths) and as this (wrongly) attributes the functions of guṇas to Ātman, one should firmly establish oneself in me who, as the fourth state, transcend those three states (and thus give up this bondage). And hereby the mind and the sense objects get (automatically) divorced.
purport:
Footnote [14]:
(The gist only) Paramātman is not affected by the three states (viz. wakefulness, dream and deep sleep). When the jīva who is a part of Paramātman’s body becomes firmly established in him, he transcends these three states and works out the divorce between the objects (guṇas) and the mind—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: अहङ्कारकृतं बन्धं आत्मनोऽर्थविपर्ययम् । विद्वान् निर्विद्य संसारचिन्तां तुर्ये स्थितः त्यजेत् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṅkārakṛtaṃ bandhaṃ ātmano vidvān nirvidya saṃsāracintāṃ turye sthitaḥ tyajet

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation: Comprehending thoroughly that bondage is caused to the Soul due to Ahaṃkāra (false identification of the body with the Soul) and that it compromises the original blissful nature of the Sou], and is thus the cause of misery, he should abhor it. He should (then) establish himself (through meditation) in the fourth state, viz. Lord Vāsudeva and give up all thoughts about sense objects (and be free from the fear of saṃsāra.

devanagari: यावत् नानार्थधीः पुंसो न निवर्तेत युक्तिभिः । जागर्ति अपि स्वपन् अज्ञः स्वप्ने जागरणं यथा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yāvat nānārthadhīḥ puṃso na nivarteta yuktibhiḥ jāgarti api svapan ajñaḥ svapne jāgaraṇaṃ yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ svap class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ svap class 2 verb]

translation: So long as the notion regarding the reality of diversity[15] (in the world) is not completely removed by means of various reasonings, the man is (as good as) asleep (i.e. ignorant)[16] though he be awake i.e. deligent in performing karmas, like unto a dreamer thinking himself awake while he is actually in a dream.
purport:
Footnote [15]:
nānārthadhīḥ—(i) Bhāgavata Candrikā: Failure to understand the difference between the body and the soul or intelligent being leading to the misconception of difference as god, man, etc.
(ii) Padaratnāvalī: The mistaken notion that the jīva is not different from Hari and Hari has become many and the jīva is an independent agent. He quotes as his authority (without naming).
bhīnnasya tveka-bhāvena tathaikasya ca bhedataḥ /
jñānaṃ nānārtha-dhīḥ proktā //

Footnote [16]:
ajña—One who cannot see the reality correctly as it is (asamyag-darśī)

devanagari: असत्त्वाद् आत्मनोऽन्येषां भावानां तत्कृता भिदा । गतयो हेतवश्चास्य मृषा स्वप्नदृशो यथा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: asattvād ātmano gatayo hetavaścāsya mṛṣā svapnadṛśo yathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: All objects other than the self (e.g. the physical body) being unreal, the differences created by them (for example difference in varṇas, castes and Āśramas—stages in human life), the goals promised (e.g. godhood in the heaven) and their causes (viz. acts prescribed in the Vedas for attaining these goals) are all unreal and non-existent like scenes in a dream.

devanagari: यो जागरे बहिरनुक्षणधर्मिणोऽर्थान् । भुङ्क्ते समस्तकरणैहृदि तत्सदृक्षान् । स्वप्ने सुषुप्त उपसंहरते स एकः । स्मृत्यन्वयात् त्रिगुणवृत्त...
english_devnagari: yo jāgare bahiranukṣaṇadharmiṇo bhuṅkte samastakaraṇaihṛdi tatsadṛkṣān svapne suṣupta upasaṃharate sa ekaḥ smṛtyanvayāt triguṇavṛttidṛgindriyeśaḥ
translation: [17] He who, while in the waking state, experiences through all his cognitive and conative sense-organs the external objects which are transient and changing every moment, who, while in the dream state, enjoys mentally objects similar (to those perceived in wakeful condition) and who, in deep sleep, withdraws them all, is one and the same, the controller of all sense-organs and the witness of the three states (of wakefulness, dream and deep slumber), as he (being the common factor in these states), remembers them all.
purport:
Footnote [17]:
Padaratnāvalī: Lord Hari, present in the right eye, and known as Viśva, shows to jīva (who is ever dependent on him) various momentary things in the wakeful state. Viṣṇu is present in the throat and is the controller of senses and is called Taijasa. He shows in the dream state all objects similar to those in wakeful condition and their imprints are in the heart. In the state of deep sleep, he is present in the heart and is called Prājña. He withdraws all senses, gives the bliss of sleep to the jīva. As noted by Smṛtis as well as Śrutis, Hari, the Supreme Lord, is the Ruler of senses and the witness of the three stages of wakefulness, dream and deep slumber. Hence to regard Paramātman and jīva as identical is a mistake.

devanagari: एवं विमृश्य गुणतो मनसः त्र्यवस्था । मन्मायया मयि कृता इति निश्चितार्थाः । संछिद्य हार्दमनुमानसदुक्तितीक्ष्ण । ज्ञानासिना भजत माखिलसंशय...
english_devnagari: evaṃ vimṛśya guṇato manasaḥ tryavasthā manmāyayā mayi kṛtā iti niścitārthāḥ saṃchidya hārdamanumānasaduktitīkṣṇa jñānāsinā bhajata mākhilasaṃśayādhim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Having reasoned thus logically and arrived at the definite conclusion that these three stages of the mind (described above) are caused by the (three) guṇas (of Māyā) and are superimposed on me (as jīva) by my own deluding potency, and having cut asunder the Ego (Ahaṃkāra), the root and receptacle of all doubts (about the identity of Paramātman and the jīva) the plurality of jivas, etc.) with the sharp sword of knowledge whetted by inference (arrived at by logical concomitance and discontinuation), by precepts of the saints (as well by śruti texts (Brahma Sutras and Upaniṣads), a person should contemplate me as abiding in his heart.

devanagari: ईक्षेत विभ्रममिदं मनसो विलासं । दृष्टं विनष्टमतिलोलमलातचक्रम् । विज्ञानमेकं उरुधेव विभाति माया । स्वप्नः त्रिधा गुणविसर्गकृतो विकल्पः ...
english_devnagari: īkṣeta vibhramamidaṃ manaso vilāsaṃ dṛṣṭaṃ vinaṣṭamatilolamalātacakram vijñānamekaṃ urudheva vibhāti māyā svapnaḥ tridhā guṇavisargakṛto vikalpaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: [18] One should realize that this (phenomenal world) is simply an illusion, only a play or work of the mind, which is just apparent and evanescent, extremely unsteady like the illusion of a circle described by a revolving fire-brand. He should see through it that consciousness, though one, appears as many and that threefold difference in states brought about by the projection of the three guṇas is nothing but Māyā (illusion).
purport:
Footnote [18]:
Padaratnāvalī (Only the difference in interpretation noted) One should realize that Paramātman becomes many under different conditions is a wrong notion. To regard Vijñāna or blissful Brahman as different in different forms is a mis-apprehension. It is the wrong notion projected by the three guṇas, sins and other conditions.
(sattvādibhis tribhir guṇais tat-sargais tat-kāryaiḥ pāpādibhiḥ kṛtaḥ paryāpta- vikalpo mithyājñānam iti śeṣaḥ)

devanagari: दृष्टिं ततः प्रतिनिवर्त्य निवृत्ततृष्णः । तूष्णीं भवेत् निजसुखानुभवो निरीहः । संदृश्यते क्व च यदीदमवस्तुबुद्ध्या । त्यक्तं भ्रमाय न भव...
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭiṃ tataḥ pratinivartya nivṛttatṛṣṇaḥ tūṣṇīṃ bhavet nijasukhānubhavo nirīhaḥ saṃdṛśyate kva ca yadīdamavastubuddhyā tyaktaṃ bhramāya na bhavet smṛtirānipātāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation: Having withdrawn his eye (thoughts) from that (visible world, his body, etc.), he should rid himself of all thirsts (the eṣaṇās—desire for money, reputations, etc.) and remain inactive and silent. Thus becoming desireless, he should remain absorbed in the bliss of self-realisation, If, in spite of this, the body, etc. is perceived (when not in meditation) for sheer physical needs such as due to hunger, thirst, calls of nature), it, having been once discarded as unreal (and something other than the Ātman), will not (be powerful enough to) create any misapprehension or illusion (and disturb one’s meditation), although the memory or the impressions of the body or of the phenomenal world may remain till the death of one’s body.

devanagari: देहं च नश्वरमवस्थितमुत्थितं वा । सिद्धो न पश्यति यतोऽध्यगमत् स्वरूपम् । दैवाद्अपेतमथ दैववशाद् उपेतं । वासो यथा परिकृतं मदिरामदान्धः ॥ ...
english_devnagari: dehaṃ ca naśvaramavasthitamutthitaṃ vā siddho na paśyati yato daivādapetamatha daivavaśād upetaṃ vāso yathā parikṛtaṃ madirāmadāndhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: A person who has attained perfection in spiritual wisdom (and is absorbed in the bliss of the Soul) is not conscious whether his transient body is in the sitting posture or standing, even though by means of that very body he has realized the essential nature of his Ātman. He is unaware if his body comes or goes to any place according to his Karma or the will of the Lord, just as a man dead drunk with wine is not conscious whether his garment is on his person or has fallen off.

devanagari: देहोऽपि दैववशगः खलु कर्म यावत् । स्वारंभकं प्रतिसमीक्षत एव सासुः । तं सप्रपञ्चमधिरूढसमाधियोगः । स्वाप्नं पुनर्न भजते प्रतिबुद्धवस्तुः ...
english_devnagari: deho svāraṃbhakaṃ pratisamīkṣata eva sāsuḥ taṃ saprapañcamadhirūḍhasamādhiyogaḥ svāpnaṃ punarna bhajate pratibuddhavastuḥ
translation: So long as the fund of merits and demerits (Karmas) which has brought the body into being, is not exhausted, this body (along with its vital principle and sense-organs) definitely continues to survive, as it is under the control of the Providence. But a person who has mastered all the stages of Yoga till the final stage called Samādhi (Perfect concentration and absorption in the Ātman) and has realized Reality, does not resort to the dreamlike unreal world (his body, relation, property, etc) anymore than an awakened person to the objects in the dream.

devanagari: मयैतदुक्तं वो विप्रा गुह्यं यत् साङ्ख्ययोगयोः । जानीत माऽगतं यज्ञं युष्मद्धर्मविवक्षया ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: mayaitaduktaṃ vo viprā guhyaṃ yat sāṅkhyayogayoḥ jānīta mā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ guh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ guh class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[optative middle third single], [imperative active second plural]

translation: What has been expounded by me now to you, O, Brāhmaṇas, is the secret essence of the paths of Sāṅkhya and Yoga. Please know me to be Yajña, Lord Viṣṇu himself who has come here with the object of teaching (and explaining) to you (the secret of) the righteous path.

devanagari: अहं योगस्य साङ्ख्यस्य सत्यस्यर्तस्य तेजसः । परायणं द्विजश्रेष्ठाः श्रियः कीर्तेः दमस्य च ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ yogasya sāṅkhyasya satyasyartasya tejasaḥ parāyaṇaṃ dvijaśreṣṭhāḥ śriyaḥ kīrteḥ damasya ca
translation: I am the ultimate goal of the paths of Sāṅkhya and Yoga, of virtue (both in its theoretical and practical aspects), of prowess, of prosperity, of glory and of selfcontrol, O the best of Brāhmaṇas.

devanagari: मां भजन्ति गुणाः सर्वे निर्गुणं निरपेक्षकम् । सुहृदं प्रियमात्मानं साम्यासङ्गादयोगुणाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: māṃ bhajanti guṇāḥ sarve nirguṇaṃ nirapekṣakam suhṛdaṃ priyamātmānaṃ sāmyāsaṅgādayoguṇāḥ
translation: All excellent attributes like impartiality, non-attachment which are not caused by the qualities like sattva, rajas and tamas, find an asylum in me who am devoid of ordinary qualities and above wants, the friend (of all beings), nay the very Soul of them all.

devanagari: इति मे छिन्नसन्देहा मुनयः सनकादयः । सभाजयित्वा परया भक्त्यागृणत संस्तवैः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti me chinnasandehā munayaḥ sanakādayaḥ sabhājayitvā parayā bhaktyāgṛṇata saṃstavaiḥ
translation: In this way, sages like Sanaka and others got their doubts explained by me. Having worshipped me with intense devotion, they extolled me in hymns and praise.

devanagari: तैरहं पूजितः सम्यक् संस्तुतः परमर्षिभिः । प्रत्येयाय स्वकं धाम पश्यतः परमेष्ठिनः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tairahaṃ pūjitaḥ samyak saṃstutaḥ paramarṣibhiḥ pratyeyāya svakaṃ dhāma paśyataḥ parameṣṭhinaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation:
Duly worshipped by them and celebrated in hymns by the great sages, I returned to my own region while Brahmā and other sages were just witnessing.
[back to top]
